definitions.text: The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
examples: The Bristol Channel has some of the world's largest tides.
A lot of driftwood was brought in on the tide.

definitions.text: Any similar gravitational effect on Earth or other body.
examples: As well as sea tides, there are much smaller land tides.
By far the largest tides on Europa are those caused by the gravitational attraction of Jupiter.

definitions.text: To cause to float with the tide; to drive or carry with the tide or stream.
To carry over or through a problem or difficulty.
To pour a tide or flood.
